e_visa.notes: Ordinary Indian passport holders must apply for an eVisa in advance via evisa.gov.mz. India appears in the portal's eVisa-eligible country list (flag listed in eVisa section); India is NOT visa-free, NOT eligible for ETA, and NOT a restricted country. Tourist and Business eVisas permit up to 90 days. An aggregator claim of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for India is INCORRECT per the official portal (verified 2026-06-14). Only Indian diplomatic/official passports may be exempt under certain bilateral arrangements, but ordinary passport holders must obtain an eVisa.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not listed as visa-free or ETA, except restricted countries
e_visa.notes: The eVisa portal lists a comprehensive global country list eligible for eVisa. Visa-exempt and ETA countries may also apply for an eVisa if their stay exceeds the permitted duration or if they require a visa category other than Tourist or Business (e.g., Sports & Culture, Crew, Humanitarian, Oil & Gas Business/Work). Duration options: 30, 60, or 90 days for Tourist and Business visas. Investment Visa available for stays up to 730 days (multiple-entry) for projects totalling USD 500,000 or more. Restricted countries (Pakistan, Bangladesh, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Nepal, Somalia) must apply at the nearest Mozambican embassy and are NOT eligible for the eVisa portal. Standard processing: approximately 5 business days. ETA processing: at least 48 hours before departure.

eta.notes: All types of passport. Tourist/Business. ETA allows stay up to 30 days.
All types of passport eligible for ETA (30 days). Diplomatic/Service passport holders are also listed under Visa Exempt (30 days) on the official portal.
All types of passport eligible for ETA (30 days). Diplomatic/Service passport holders are also listed under Visa Exempt (90 days) on the official portal — see conditional_access.
All types of passport eligible for ETA (30 days). Diplomatic/Service passport holders are also listed under Visa Exempt (90 days) on the official portal — see conditional_access.

visa_policy.notes: Visa categories on evisa.gov.mz (platform relaunched 11 February 2026): (1) Visa Exempt (waiver countries) - listed in visa_free and conditional_access above; (2) Special/ETA countries listed in eta above (30-day pre-travel Electronic Travel Authorization for selected nationalities); (3) eVisa eligible — the majority of the world's countries, including all ETA and visa-exempt nationalities who need longer stays or non-Tourist/Business visa types; (4) Restricted countries (Pakistan, Bangladesh, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Nepal, Somalia) must apply at nearest Mozambican embassy and cannot use the eVisa portal. Standard eVisa processing: approximately 5 business days. ETA processing: at least 48 hours before departure. Tourist and Business eVisas permit stays up to 90 days; Investment Visa up to 730 days (multiple-entry).

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Argentina only: visa-free entry for up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Bilateral agreement: 'Agreement between the Argentine Republic and the Republic of Mozambique on the Suppression of Visas for Holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports', dated 5 October 2013. Ordinary Argentine passport holders require eVisa.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Brazil only: visa-free entry for up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Covered under CPLP multilateral agreement. Ordinary Brazilian passport holders require eVisa.
Diplomatic, Official and Service passport holders of Cuba only: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary Cuban passport holders require eVisa.
Diplomatic, Service & Special passport holders of Egypt only: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary Egyptian passport holders require eVisa.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Guinea Bissau only: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Covered under CPLP multilateral agreement. Ordinary Guinea Bissau passport holders require eVisa.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Indonesia: visa-free entry up to 30 days, Tourist & Business. All Indonesian passport types also eligible for ETA (30 days). Ordinary Indonesian passports use the ETA pathway, not the Visa Exempt pathway.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Portugal: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Covered under CPLP multilateral agreement. All Portuguese passport types also eligible for ETA (30 days). Ordinary Portuguese passports use the ETA pathway.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Sao Tome & Principe: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary passport holders: visa-free up to 30 days (see visa_free). Covered under CPLP multilateral agreement.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of South Africa: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary passport holders: visa-free up to 30 days (see visa_free). Bilateral agreement came into force 29 April 2005.

Diplomatic and Service passport holders of South Korea: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. All South Korean passport types also eligible for ETA (30 days). Ordinary South Korean passports use the ETA pathway.
Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Thailand only: visa-free entry up to 30 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary Thai passport holders require eVisa.
Diplomatic, Service & Special passport holders of Turkey only: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary Turkish passport holders require eVisa.
Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Venezuela only: visa-free entry up to 30 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary Venezuelan passport holders require eVisa.
Diplomatic and Service passport holders of Vietnam only: visa-free entry up to 90 days, Tourist & Business. Ordinary Vietnamese passport holders require eVisa.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Mozambican government domain. No official government page found confirming a CBI program. Non-official/aggregator sources (goldenvisas.com) state no CBI program exists in Mozambique.

programs.name: Investment Visa (eVisa)
programs.notes: Available via official eVisa portal. Applicable for travel to invest in a project totalling a value equal or greater than USD 500,000. Duration options: 90, 180, 365 or 730 days (multiple-entry). Governed by Law No. 23/2022 of December 29. Processing time: 3 to 5 business days. This is an entry/investment visa, not a formal long-term residency-by-investment or golden visa program; it is a precursor to undertaking investment in Mozambique. No path to PR or citizenship specified on the official portal.

agreements: SADC (Southern African Development Community) member state
CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) member state — multilateral diplomatic/service/special passport visa suppression agreement among CPLP members

validation_notes: ADDED explicit India (IND) entry to e_visa array on 2026-06-14: verified against live evisa.gov.mz (official Mozambique National Immigration Service portal); India's flag appears in the eVisa-eligible section and is absent from visa_free, ETA, and restricted-country lists — ordinary Indian passport holders require an e-visa applied for in advance; aggregator claim of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for India is FALSE per the official source.
Re-verified against live evisa.gov.mz on 2026-06-13 via Firecrawl stealth scrape (HTTP 200, full page content retrieved).

REMOVED the redundant CPLP grouped entry from conditional_access that listed Angola, Brazil, Cabo Verde, Guinea Bissau, Portugal, Sao Tome & Principe together. Angola and Cabo Verde ordinary passports are visa-free, not a CPLP diplomatic special case. Individual entries for Brazil, Guinea Bissau, Portugal, Sao Tome & Principe diplomatic/service are retained and corrected.

CORRECTED visa_free nationality labels: 'Sao Tome & Principe (Ordinary)' cleaned to 'Sao Tome & Principe' (ordinary passport noted in notes field); 'South Africa (Ordinary)' cleaned to 'South Africa' (ordinary passport noted in notes field); 'Swaziland / Eswatini' changed to 'Eswatini' (primary current name) with original portal label noted.

CORRECTED eta nationality labels: Removed parenthetical '(all passport types)' and '(Ordinary)' suffixes from nationality field for Indonesia, Portugal, South Korea to use clean nationality names with clarification in notes field instead.
CORRECTED e_visa max_stay_days from null to 90: the portal confirms Tourist and Business eVisas permit up to 90 days; Investment Visa up to 730 days.

CONFIRMED Argentina conditional_access: live portal shows 'Diplomatic and Service' only (90 days) — 'official' passport type removed from passport_types as portal does not list it for Argentina (only Cuba uses 'official').
CONFIRMED investment visa minimum: USD 500,000, durations 90/180/365/730 days, multiple-entry, processing 3-5 business days.
CONFIRMED restricted countries: Pakistan, Bangladesh, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Nepal, Somalia — unchanged.
CONFIRMED ETA processing: at least 48 hours before departure. eVisa standard: approximately 5 business days.
ADDED pae.gov.mz to official_domains (PAE is Mozambique's Agency for Business Environment, publishes official visa policy announcements on government domain).
ADDED CPLP agreement to agreements array.
gaps: The eVisa portal's eVisa-eligible section lists a comprehensive global country list but does not individually enumerate stay durations per country — only that they are eligible for eVisa (specific approved duration varies by visa type applied for).

Restricted countries (Pakistan, Bangladesh, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Nepal, Somalia) are noted as requiring embassy-based visa applications; no further details (fees, processing times) found on official sources.

No official government page found confirming a formal citizenship-by-investment or golden visa/residency-by-investment program. Media sources (November 2025) mention a proposed tiered investor residency (USD 500,000 for 5 years, USD 5,000,000 for 10 years) but no official government page confirms this program is live.
No official digital nomad, skilled worker fast-track, or startup visa program found on official Mozambican government domains.

SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons: Mozambique is a SADC member but it is unclear from official sources whether Mozambique has fully ratified the free movement protocol; practical bilateral visa-free arrangements are captured in the visa_free list.

TYPE A (held third-country credential): No official Mozambican government source found granting visa-free, VoA, ETA, or expedited eVisa entry based on holding a valid US, Schengen, UK, UAE, GCC, Australian, Canadian, or any other foreign visa or residence permit.
TYPE B (special status): No official Mozambican source mentions India OCI card, or any equivalent diaspora/heritage status, as conferring conditional entry.

Bilateral diplomatic/service passport agreement texts for Egypt, Turkey, Thailand, Venezuela, Vietnam bilateral waivers were not found on official Mozambican government domains; their presence on evisa.gov.mz waiver table is taken as authoritative confirmation.
The senami.gov.mz domain redirects to visitos.senami.gov.mz which may contain additional immigration details not checked in this validation pass.

outbound_official.note: no official Mozambique government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The MINEC (Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation) at minec.gov.mz publishes a 'Vistos de Entrada' page listing countries whose nationals may enter Mozambique without a visa (inbound), but no equivalent outbound page advising Mozambican ordinary passport holders where they may travel without a prior consular visa was found on any official Mozambican government domain (minec.gov.mz, senami.gov.mz, pae.gov.mz, evisa.gov.mz, or any overseas embassy site). Searched 2026-06-14.
